verbatim_text: Ask them what they pay for. Ask them if they'd be really bummed if your company went away. Ask them what would make them recommend the product to their friends. And ask them if they've recommended it to any yet. You should make this feedback loop as tight as possible. If your product gets 10% better every week, that compounds really, really quickly. One of the great advantages of start, of software startups is just how short you can make the feedback loop. It can go circle in hours, and the best companies usually have the tightest feedback loops. You should try to keep this going for all of your company's life. But it's really important in the early days. The good news is that all of this is doable. It's hard, it takes a lot of effort, but there's no magic. The plan is at least straight forward and you will eventually get to a great product. Great founders don't put anyone between themselves and their users.
